title: Image pull resolution problem with short name
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:
>
/kind bug
>
> /kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
A basic pull of anything from docker.io fails. It appears to be a pull resolution problem, but not familiar with atomic-registries (suspect) to troubleshoot much deeper.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Clone repository

2. Build and install

3. Verify /usr/local/etc/registries.conf actually has docker.io listed

4. Run pull command: podman pull fedora

**Describe the results you received:**

podman pull alpine
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/alpine:latest...
Failed
error pulling image "alpine": error pulling image from "alpine": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/alpine:latest: unknown: Not Found

podman pull fedora
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/fedora:latest...
Failed
error pulling image "fedora": error pulling image from "fedora": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/fedora:latest: unknown: Not Found


**Describe the results you expected:**

Should be similar to 
podman pull rhel
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/rhel:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:9a32f102e6778e4b3c677f1f93fa121808006d3c9e002237677248de9acb9589
 4.50 MB / ? [-----------------=----------------------------------------------] 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.2
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Git Commit:    "e814936915d28286fa88f1211bc354687a358a7c"
Built:         Thu Feb 15 05:25:24 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
RHEL 7.4
cat /etc/redhat-release 
Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server release 7.4 (Maipo)
[root@rhel7 ~]# uname -a
Linux rhel7.dc2.crunchtools.com 3.10.0-693.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Jul 6 19:56:57 EDT 2017 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

comments.body:
What does your /etc/containers/registries.conf file have in it?
[registries.search]
registries = ['docker.io', 'registry.fedoraproject.org', 'registry.access.redhat
.com']
@fatherlinux jump onto #podman on freenode and we can cooperatively debug this ....

Yup, per my other issue, it looks right to me :-)


cat /usr/local/etc/registries.conf
[registries.search]
registries = ['registry.access.redhat.com', 
'registry.fedoraproject.org', 'docker.io']

[registries.insecure]
registries = []

#blocked (docker only)
[registries.block]
registries = []


I ran a yum update and picked up a BUNCH of changes, in hopes that 
"magic" might just happen. Still no love - same thing:


podman pull fedora
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/fedora:latest...
Failed
error pulling image "fedora": error pulling image from "fedora": Error 
determining manifest MIME type for 
docker://registry.access.redhat.com/fedora:latest: unknown: Not Found


Best Regards

Scott M


On 02/15/2018 11:00 AM, Daniel J Walsh wrote:
>
> What does your /etc/containers/registries.conf file have in it?
> [registries.search]
> registries = ['docker.io', 'registry.fedoraproject.org', 
> 'registry.access.redhat
> .com']
